rulings.Case Title: PATRICK BURKE, ET AL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cummins Engine Company Sued Erroneously Herein As Cummins Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Having Subject Matter Of Request For Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Cummins Engine Company¿s motion to compel responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and request for production of documents and having subject matter of request for admissions deemed admitted and for sanctions is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant is awarded sanctions of $1072.50. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Further Deposition Of E.I. Du Pont De Nemours And Companys Person Most Knowledgeable-Custodian Of Records And Production Of Documents And Things
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the further deposition of E.I. Du Pont De Nemours and Company¿s person most knowledgeable-custodian of records and production of documents was heard on March 10. If all issues raised by the motion have been resolved, the parties must inform the court by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. If all issues have not been resolved, then by 4:30 pm on April 11, the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, which issues remain in dispute and then appear at the hearing on April 12. = (220/HEK)
